title: alsine tenuifolia subsp. lydia
intro: Alsine tenuifolia subsp. lydia, also known as lyre-leaved stonecrop or Lydia's stonecrop, is a subspecies of the Alsine tenuifolia plant, a flowering plant in the saxifrage family. It is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Alsine tenuifolia subsp. lydia include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as having potential effects on the nervous system and immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often used in traditional folk medicine in certain regions.
